deployment_question: Does the model predict perturbation-specific effects beyond systematic variation, batch, or background cell state?
definition: Hold out or stratify by systematic variation sources such as batch, donor, cell cycle, dataset, background state, or perturbation-effect magnitude to test whether the model learns perturbation-specific signal.

split_operator.spectra_mapping: targets an operating point where confounder overlap is reduced or response signal differs

implementation_recipe: Identify systematic variation sources and perturbation-specific effect measures.
Construct held-out confounder levels or effect bins.
Report performance separately for perturbation-specific signal and systematic background.
quality_gates: Include simple baselines that exploit only systematic variation.
Report metrics sensitive to perturbation-specific effects.
Check whether preprocessing removes or amplifies confounders.
failure_modes: A model can perform well by predicting batch or baseline state.
Effect-size bins may induce label distribution shift.

citations.title: Benchmarking foundation cell models for post-perturbation RNA-seq prediction
citations.evidence: Finds simple baselines outperform foundation models and notes low perturbation-specific variance in benchmark datasets.

citations.title: Evaluating Single-Cell Perturbation Response Models Is Far from Straightforward
citations.evidence: Argues existing evaluation metrics can misrepresent perturbation-response model performance.
